name: Studio Ghibli
description: Wonder, courage, and quiet wisdom from the enchanted films of Studio Ghibli.
author: Quips Editorial

quotes.content: Once you've met someone, you never really forget them. It just takes a while for your memories to return.
quotes.notes: Zeniba reassures Chihiro about her fading memories; wording is the Disney English dub (John Lasseter production).

quotes.content: If you completely forget your name, you'll never find your way back home.
quotes.notes: Haku warns Chihiro after Yubaba steals her name; Disney English dub wording.

quotes.content: So, your name's Chihiro? What a pretty name. And it belongs to me now.
quotes.notes: Yubaba seizes Chihiro's name to bind her in servitude; Disney English dub.

quotes.content: Now go, and don't look back.
quotes.notes: Haku's parting instruction as Chihiro leaves the spirit world; Disney English dub.

quotes.content: To see with eyes unclouded by hate.
quotes.notes: Ashitaka's guiding creed for the film; Neil Gaiman's Miramax English dub (Japanese literally closer to 'to see with clear eyes').

quotes.content: The trees cry out as they die, but you cannot hear them.
quotes.notes: The wolf-god Moro rebukes humankind; Neil Gaiman's Miramax English dub.

quotes.content: Now watch closely, everyone. I'm going to show you how to kill a god.
quotes.notes: Eboshi before firing on the Forest Spirit; Miramax English dub wording.

quotes.content: No. Live.
quotes.notes: Ashitaka urging the dying San to keep living; Miramax English dub.

quotes.content: You cannot alter your fate. However, you can rise to meet it if you choose.
quotes.notes: The village wise-woman sends the cursed Ashitaka away; Miramax English dub (minor wording variants like 'change your fate' appear in fan transcripts).

quotes.content: A heart is a heavy burden.
quotes.notes: Sophie on Howl's traded-away heart; Disney English dub.

quotes.content: I see no point in living if I can't be beautiful.
quotes.notes: Howl's melodramatic despair after his hair is dyed the wrong color; Disney English dub (Christian Bale).

quotes.content: I'm done running away. I've finally found someone I want to protect. You.
quotes.notes: Howl's declaration to Sophie; wording axis failed - the Disney dub phrasing varies across transcripts ('now that I have something I want to protect - it's you').

quotes.content: May all your bacon burn!
quotes.notes: The fire demon Calcifer's petulant curse; Disney English dub (Billy Crystal).

quotes.content: Without even thinking about it, I used to be able to fly. Now I'm trying to look inside myself and find out how I did it.
quotes.notes: Kiki describing her lost magic; Disney English dub (Kirsten Dunst).

quotes.content: We each need to find our own inspiration, Kiki. Sometimes it's not easy.
quotes.notes: The painter Ursula's advice on creative blocks; wording axis failed - reconstructed from fan summaries rather than a confirmed dub transcript.

quotes.content: All right, first: don't panic! Second: don't panic! And third: did I mention not to panic?
quotes.notes: The cat Jiji's frantic pep talk; Disney English dub (Phil Hartman, whose ad-libs differ notably from the subtitles).

quotes.content: Take root in the ground, live in harmony with the wind, plant your seeds in the winter, and rejoice with the birds in the coming of spring.
quotes.notes: Sheeta recites the Spell of the Valley to Muska; Disney English dub (Anna Paquin).

quotes.content: No matter how many weapons you have, no matter how great your technology might be, the world cannot live without love.
quotes.notes: Sheeta explaining why the Laputans abandoned their floating kingdom; Disney English dub.

quotes.content: He will descend from the sky, clad in blue and surrounded by fields of gold, to restore mankind's bond with the earth.
quotes.authorName: Obaba (Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind)
quotes.source: Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind (1984)
quotes.notes: The old woman's prophecy fulfilled by Nausicaa; wording axis failed - the Disney 2005 dub phrasing varies and this is a normalized paraphrase.

quotes.content: Airplanes are beautiful dreams. Engineers turn dreams into reality.
quotes.notes: The Italian designer Caproni in Jiro's dream; Disney English dub (Stanley Tucci) delivers these as two adjacent lines.

quotes.content: The wind is rising! We must try to live!
quotes.notes: The film's refrain, a line from Paul Valery's poem 'Le Cimetiere marin'; Disney English dub.

quotes.content: Life is wonderful, isn't it?
quotes.notes: Naoko to Jiro amid her illness; Disney English dub (Emily Blunt).

quotes.content: Ponyo loves Sosuke! I will be a human, too!
quotes.notes: The fish-girl Ponyo declaring her wish; Disney English dub (Noah Cyrus).

quotes.content: Ponyo wants ham!
quotes.notes: Ponyo's gleeful demand at dinner; Disney English dub.

quotes.content: You know, a long time ago, trees and people used to be good friends.
quotes.notes: The father explaining why he bought the house by the camphor tree; wording axis failed - the Disney 2006 and older Streamline dubs phrase this differently.

quotes.content: I would like to make a film to tell children it's good to be alive.
quotes.notes: Miyazaki's stated aim, widely attributed to a making-of interview around My Neighbor Totoro; not film dialogue.

quotes.content: Today, all of humanity's dreams are cursed somehow.
quotes.notes: Miyazaki reflecting on The Wind Rises in Mami Sunada's documentary 'The Kingdom of Dreams and Madness'; English subtitle translation.

quotes.content: Yes, I'm the scariest witch of them all - the kind that cleans!
quotes.notes: Sophie briskly taking charge of Howl's filthy castle; Disney English dub.
